narrative.headline: What 2017 Jeep Renegade owners report most
narrative.summary: Engine complaints dominate reports for this model year, with the most common pattern being unexpected stalling or power loss while driving, sometimes at highway speeds. Electrical system faults and fuel or propulsion issues also appear frequently, often producing similar symptoms of sudden loss of power or difficulty starting. Across all four component areas, owners describe failures that occurred without warning, in some cases during active driving maneuvers.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Owners report the vehicle stalling without warning while driving at speed, requiring dealer service visits that did not always resolve the condition. · 3 fire · 2 crash · 3 injured

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Owners describe sudden, complete power loss occurring in the middle of active driving, including during turns at intersections, with some incidents happening on nearly new vehicles. · 4 fire · 3 crash

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: Owners report difficulty starting after refueling, accompanied by intense knocking noises and violent sputtering severe enough to shake the entire vehicle. · 1 crash

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: Owners describe sudden power loss while the vehicle is in motion, including during low-speed turning maneuvers at intersections on vehicles with very few miles. · 3 crash
